mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of observations where PAYEMS is increasing.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6 percent signals deficient aggregate demand and rising credit risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 6%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1 percent, negative real interest rates drive capital reallocation from cash to equities, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where industrial production is near peak capacity, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals an impending earnings contraction and predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO month-over-month declines occur during late-cycle expansions, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand that translates directly into corporate revenue growth, predicting positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of weekly observations over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is raised by more than 25 basis points within a single month, the sudden tightening of liquidity conditions triggers a repricing of risk assets, causing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following a month-over-month FEDFUNDS increase greater than 0.25 percentage points are negative in fewer than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling retail sales (RSAFS) creates a recessionary demand-labor feedback loop that amplifies downside earnings revisions, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving factory utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.
